description: Duty drawback qualification for Forge v4. Identifies companies that import physical goods into the US AND export/ship internationally, making them eligible for duty drawback refunds.

categories.name: HIGH_CONFIDENCE
categories.description: Strong signals for BOTH import into US AND international shipping/export. Clear evidence of overseas manufacturing/sourcing AND ships outside US (beyond just Canada/Mexico).

categories.name: MEDIUM_CONFIDENCE
categories.description: Likely qualifies - has signals for BOTH import AND export, but one or both rely on industry inference rather than explicit statements.

categories.name: DISQUALIFIED
categories.description: Does not meet criteria: no physical products, confirmed no imports (US-made only), confirmed no international shipping, only ships to Canada/Mexico, or is a pure retailer buying from US distributors.

products_found.description: 3-6 specific physical products found (empty if none)

import_signals.description: What signals suggest imports? Include both explicit evidence AND industry inference

export_signals.description: What signals suggest exports? Must be to countries OTHER than just Canada/Mexico

industry_category.description: Industry category for inference: apparel, footwear, electronics, tools, furniture, beauty, jewelry, other

questions.question: Does this company sell physical, tangible products that can be shipped?
questions.guidance: YES if: product catalogs, shop pages, add-to-cart buttons, product images showing shoes, apparel, electronics, tools, etc. ALSO YES if: shipbuilders (build ships/vessels/boats), heavy machinery manufacturers, industrial equipment makers, construction of physical items for clients - these are physical products even without e-commerce. NO if: pure services (consulting, software development), digital products only, no physical output.

questions.question: Does this company likely IMPORT goods into the United States?
questions.guidance:
CRITICAL: You MUST apply industry inference if no explicit import evidence is found.

EXPLICIT SIGNALS (answer YES immediately): 'Made in China/Vietnam/Mexico/overseas', 'sourced from', 'manufactured overseas', 'imported', 'Japanese product', 'German-made'.

INDUSTRY INFERENCE (answer YES if in these categories AND no 'Made in USA' claim):
- Apparel/fashion/clothing brands = YES (almost all imported)
- Formal dresses/gowns/prom dresses = YES (imported)
- Footwear/shoes = YES (almost always imported)
- Electronics = YES (imported)
- German/Japanese/European specialty tools = YES (imported)
- Automotive tools for VW/Audi/BMW = YES (German tools, imported)
- Beauty/cosmetics = YES (ingredients imported)
- Jewelry = YES (materials imported)
- Furniture without US factory = YES (likely imported)

NEGATIVE SIGNALS (answer NO): 'Made in USA', 'manufactured in our US facility', 'handcrafted in [US state]', print-on-demand, screen printing, local fabrication, embroidery done in-house.

NOTE: Being a retailer stocking brands is NOT importing. Only brand owners/direct importers count.

questions.question: Does this company ship/export goods OUTSIDE the United States to countries OTHER than just Canada and Mexico?
questions.guidance:
IMPORTANT: Shipping ONLY to Canada and/or Mexico does NOT count as international for duty drawback purposes.

YES if: 'ships worldwide', 'international shipping' to Europe/Asia/etc, shipping page lists countries beyond North America, multi-currency checkout (EUR, GBP, etc).

INDUSTRY INFERENCE FOR EXPORTS (answer YES if no explicit shipping info found):
- Fashion/apparel/formal dress brands with confirmed imports → YES (fashion brands almost always ship globally)
- Shipbuilders/vessel manufacturers → YES (ships are exported globally)
- Specialty tool importers (German/Japanese tools) → YES (specialty markets ship worldwide)
- E-commerce sites with Shopify/standard platforms → LEAN YES (most enable international shipping)

NO if: US-only shipping explicitly stated, OR only ships to Canada/Mexico and no other countries mentioned, OR local service business.

LOOK FOR: Shipping page, FAQ, footer, checkout process. If no explicit shipping info AND company is in globally-traded industry, use inference.

questions.question: Is this company a BRAND OWNER (sells their own branded products) or DIRECT IMPORTER (imports products themselves)?
questions.guidance:
YES if: Has their own product line with their brand name on products, custom designs, mentions overseas suppliers/factories, manufacturing partners.

ALSO YES for multi-brand retailers if: They stock international/niche brands not commonly found in US retail (likely direct importers), OR they import products directly from overseas even if third-party brands, OR they specialize in products from specific countries (e.g., Korean beauty, Japanese tools, European fashion).

NO ONLY if: They explicitly source from US distributors/wholesalers, says 'handcrafted in [US state]', explicitly US-manufactured, OR they only stock mainstream brands widely available through US distribution (Nike, Adidas, etc).

IMPORTANT: If they claim US-made/handcrafted in a US location, answer NO for import purposes even if they are technically a brand owner. Multi-brand retailers CAN qualify if they are direct importers.

questions.question: What specific signals suggest this company imports goods into the US? Include both explicit evidence AND industry inference reasoning.
What specific signals suggest this company ships internationally (beyond Canada/Mexico)?

classification_logic.description: Classification based on import/export signals. BOTH import AND export required. Use industry inference for imports when no explicit evidence.

apply_in_order.note: Requires BOTH - cannot qualify with only one signal

classification_logic.internal_test: Does this company BOTH (1) import goods into the US AND (2) ship those goods to countries outside US/Canada/Mexico?

sufficiency_check.prompt_addition: CRITICAL: Apply industry inference for imports! If a company sells apparel/fashion/dresses/footwear/tools/electronics AND has no 'Made in USA' claim, mark likely_imports_to_us as TRUE with industry inference reasoning.

page_priority.description: Prioritize pages that reveal import/export activity

industry_inference.description: MANDATORY: Apply these rules when no explicit import evidence is found. If industry matches AND no 'Made in USA' claim, mark imports as TRUE.
likely_imports: Apparel/fashion brands - mark imports=TRUE unless explicitly 'Made in USA' with factory proof
Formal dresses/gowns/prom dresses - mark imports=TRUE (almost always imported)
Electronics and consumer electronics - mark imports=TRUE
Eyewear frames - mark imports=TRUE (almost always imported)
Automotive parts/tools for German/Japanese cars (VW/Audi/BMW/Mercedes) - mark imports=TRUE
Specialty tools (German, Japanese, Swiss precision tools) - mark imports=TRUE
Furniture without US factory mentioned - mark imports=TRUE
Sporting goods and outdoor equipment - mark imports=TRUE
Beauty and cosmetics - mark imports=TRUE (ingredients imported)
likely_us_made: Screen printing / embroidery services - mark imports=FALSE
Local fabrication/welding with US materials - mark imports=FALSE
Companies explicitly stating 'Made in USA', 'handcrafted in [US location]' - mark imports=FALSE

qualified.reasoning: Apparel brand (industry inference: imports=TRUE), ships to Europe (exports=TRUE)
VW/Audi specialty tools (German = industry inference: imports=TRUE), ships worldwide (exports=TRUE)
Formal dresses/gowns (industry inference: imports=TRUE), international shipping available (exports=TRUE)

disqualified.reasoning: Screen printing in Kansas = US-made, no imports
'Handcrafted in Rhode Island' = US-made, no imports even though jewelry industry
Only ships to Canada/US - Canada doesn't count for duty drawback
Imports from China but US-only shipping - needs BOTH import AND export

edge_cases.scenario: Company ships to Canada AND Europe
edge_cases.handling: QUALIFIED - Europe counts as international even if also ships to Canada

edge_cases.scenario: Apparel brand that claims 'handcrafted in California'
edge_cases.handling: DISQUALIFIED - explicit US-made claim overrides industry inference

edge_cases.scenario: Tool company with no origin info but sells VW/Audi tools
edge_cases.handling: QUALIFIED via industry inference - German car specialty tools are imported
